title: 2026 Semiconductor Supply Chain Report
content: The 2026 global semiconductor market size reached 780 billion USD, with AI chips growing 28%. In advanced processes (3nm and below), only 18 fabs worldwide are capable. TSMC holds a dominant share of the global advanced process foundry market at 62%, Samsung at 15%, Intel at 8%. In mature processes (28nm and above), Chinese domestic capacity saw YoY growth of 35%. HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) demand surged, with SK Hynix holding 95% market share and a 24-month order backlog.

title: 2026 Chip Export Restrictions Update
content: In February 2026, updated chip export controls were announced, including: 1) AI chip export threshold lowered to 300 TOPS; 2) EDA tools for GAA architecture restricted; 3) HBM exports now require individual approval. ASML announced a suspension of all DUV exports to China. An additional 23 semiconductor entities were added to the restricted list. In response, China accelerates domestic substitution; Huawei's Ascend 910C chip has reached approximately 85% of the A100's performance level.
